title: Chapter Thirty-Four: Thieving Ghost
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"Amusing fellow. How dare you come right at me."

Thieving Ghost's mouth corners rose. Yeon-shin paid no attention and kept walking.

He had heard about the man. A master of grappling arts. His title came not from fighting like a ghost, but from stealing like one -- techniques, weapons, limbs. Anything.

"Do you know the meaning of my title?"

Yeon-shin did not answer. Five paces now. He could see the calluses on the man's remaining hand, opening and closing.

"I am Thieving Ghost because I take anything. I took your senior brother's foolish eye too."

"Senior brother?"

"Not senior brother, I suppose. Desolate Sect has no hierarchy, does it? Unable to call masters *master*. The Radiant Demon Wing Lord who taught you must be wandering aimlessly through the mountains."

"What is your purpose? Did you not take the Great Violet Pill?"

Energy flowed from the stump of Thieving Ghost's missing left arm.

"The one you cannot call senior brother. Blue Eyes Demon Sword's neck."

*He came back for Cheong Myeong. Not the pill.*

"You risk the Mount Hua Sword Saint and the Squad Leader returning early?"

"You do not understand the meaning a martial artist's hands hold." Irritation flickered across Thieving Ghost's face. "Fine. They said you were quite promising, but you are no different from the ignorant ones."

Yeon-shin stopped. The distance between them was close enough for a single leap to end it.

"Child of the Jeong family," Thieving Ghost said. "You buried your family in the back mountain, did you not? I wonder what has become of that place. They dug up the mountain. And finally burned it all."

Yeon-shin adjusted his grip.

He remembered the servant who called him for breakfast each morning. His father, who had finally acknowledged his son at the end. The family members who had kept their distance all his life, approaching in death with love and hatred intertwined.

The door rattled. He held it shut.

"I will bury you along with Blue Eyes Demon Sword's remaining eye," Thieving Ghost said.

The moment he mentioned Cheong Myeong's eye, something shifted inside Yeon-shin's mind.

*When do martial artists despair?*

When their sect, their brothers, or they themselves face death.

When they can no longer practice martial arts.

And -- deeper than both -- when the life's work they have built with their hands is made meaningless before their eyes.

Revenge. The emotion right after the desire for survival.

Yeon-shin's crown blazed. The Jeong Family Dynamic Technique surged. Radiant Sword Style incantations unspooled from his mind like a scroll unfurling. True energy bled from his hand like sunlight bleeding through storm clouds.

The Desolate Sword struck upward.

It missed.

The first time since he had created the technique.

Thieving Ghost's body technique was exactly as his title suggested -- ghostlike, his body turning in mid-rush with fluid, terrifying grace. His remaining hand curved outward, energy wave flowing along it in a serpentine pattern.

Grappling Hold. Advanced. Aimed at Yeon-shin's sword hand.

*His technique was designed to seize and steal.*

Yeon-shin twisted his body low, ducked beneath the grab, and drove forward with a full-body strike. Wind spiralled around him. The Eternal Blossom Fist's rotational force combined with Cheong Myeong's teachings destroyed any possibility of being caught from behind.

Thieving Ghost stepped back, eyes narrowing.

"An Elf clan's body technique? No -- only the form is similar."

"You talk too much."

"The Blood Flame Sect is a fearsome force," Thieving Ghost continued. "They respond to sprouts that could become great enemies. They send assassins and dig up the past. They uncovered everything -- that you are from the Jeong family annihilated in Xinye County."

Yeon-shin said nothing. His energy rose again.

"A youngster would kill me?"

The moment hung.

Then Yeon-shin sheathed the Desolate Sword.

The blade slid home with a hiss. He stepped forward empty-handed.

His right hand extended -- mimicking the Grappling Hold perfectly. The same energy pattern, the same serpentine curve, but refined. Elevated to the realm of rising martial arts.

Later start. Earlier finish.

His hand caught Thieving Ghost's wrist before the man's grab could close. The Jeong Family Dynamic Technique's supernatural strength locked the joint. Sealed every possibility of movement with crushing force.

"You -- how could you --!"

Cold sweat erupted on Thieving Ghost's face. The confidence of moments ago had evaporated. He had watched his unique martial art stolen and reborn in better form in the enemy's hands.

"This seems better," Yeon-shin said. "Your technique was not that advanced."

Terror. That was the word for what was spreading across Thieving Ghost's face.

"Shall I teach you the incantation?"

The man flinched. Understanding broke across his features. From longing to despair.

*This is it. The way to break them.*

Yeon-shin crushed the wrist.

While Thieving Ghost screamed, Yeon-shin drew the Desolate Sword with his left hand in reverse grip and cut him down.

---

He carried the head upstairs.

The inn was half destroyed. The Blood Flame Sect fighters who had attacked alongside Thieving Ghost were already dealt with -- Ma Jin had returned and, with the Mount Hua Sect masters and Radiant Demon Wing, had swept the rest clean.

Ma Jin stared at the head in Yeon-shin's hand. Then at the wooden box he held in the other -- taken from Thieving Ghost's body.

Inside: the Great Violet Pill. Pure white. Energy unlike anything Yeon-shin had ever felt.

"You..." Ma Jin's scarred face twitched. "Blue level will not be far off. The shortest time. The youngest."

"There seems to be something more urgent."

"Yes. The Mount Hua Sect has been hit."

---

Yu Hyeon lay face-down on the bed, still as death.

The Mount Hua Sect's head disciple. The bold one who had promised to visit Desolate Sect. Now his true energy was completely spent -- his dantian emptied to the dregs.

Cheon Ju, the Mount Hua Sword Saint, stood at the bedside. His sharp features had softened with something close to grief.

"He used everything protecting his senior brother. The Great Violet Pill's energy could substitute for the Purple Mist Divine Technique he practises, but dissolving the pill's energy must be done delicately. The half hour he has left is far from enough."

Ma Jin's lips moved. Sending a telepathic message. Cheon Ju's eyes widened as they turned to Yeon-shin.

"Did you get permission for me to do it?" Yeon-shin asked.

Ma Jin nodded. "Yes. Swallow it now. This too will become your achievement."

Yeon-shin laid Yu Hyeon face-down. Sat cross-legged beside him. Took the pure white pill from the box and swallowed it.

A fragrant scent. The pill dissolved the instant it reached his body.

*So there are miraculous medicines that dissolve immediately.*

He moved his true energy from the lower dantian. The Great Violet Pill crumbled within the major heavenly circuit, and its energy began to rise -- enormous, layered, dense. Power reserved for Mount Hua's finest.

He used it. Not to strengthen himself, but to push open the middle dantian further. The Jeong Family Dynamic Technique's true energy became sticky and heavy as it passed through -- endurance, exactly as Baek Mi-ryeo had described.

*Half opened. More will require my own true energy.*

Enough. He placed his palm on the centre of Yu Hyeon's back and began pouring the Great Violet Pill's energy into the boy's body.

"Already...!" Cheon Ju breathed.

"Yu Hyeon. Get up."

Even speaking mid-transfer, his energy did not waver. The middle dantian held everything steady.

He sealed the mute acupoint at Yu Hyeon's nape, stimulated the boy's interior with circulating energy until consciousness had no choice but to surface, then spoke again.

"This is the Great Violet Pill's energy. I will give you all of it. Operate the Purple Mist Divine Technique."

He felt Yu Hyeon's pulse rise. Felt the boy's turbulent emotions at his fingertips. Then he transferred everything and removed his hand.

He stood without difficulty. Ma Jin's hand, reaching to steady him, pressed empty air.

"What are you doing?"

"Nothing."

Silence as they waited. When Yu Hyeon finally rose, Cheon Ju seized his wrist and checked his pulse. The Sword Saint's eyes widened until they seemed they would tear.

"You completely transferred this much dissolved energy? Such a thing..."

Shock. Admiration. The expression of a supreme master who had seen the impossible twice in one day.

"You are truly a great person!"

"I understood you trusted me with it."

There was nothing more to say. The Great Violet Pill had returned to the one it was meant for. The mission was complete.


---
